# Next Tasks Queue - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Pre-Development Planning  
**Last Updated:** July 2025

---

## Immediate Priority (Next Phase)

### 🏗️ 1. High-Precision Clock Implementation (KER-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 1 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- [ ] Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- [ ] Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- [ ] Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/timing/
├── src/
│   ├── PrecisionClock.ts
│   ├── TimingDriftDetector.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
└── package.json
```

**Dependencies**: Nx monorepo setup, testing infrastructure  
**Risk Level**: High (foundational for entire platform)  
**Notes**: This is the critical first implementation for ≤2ms timing precision

### ✅ 2. Create Development Team Job Descriptions
**Status**: Completed  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Completed**: July 8, 2025  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Job Description Requirements:**
- [x] **Senior Frontend Developer** - TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL/PixiJS, timing precision expertise
- [x] **Senior Backend Developer** - Node.js, PostgreSQL, API design, scientific data handling
- [x] **UI/UX Designer** - Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), scientific workflows, Figma
- [x] **DevOps Engineer** - Kubernetes, CI/CD, monitoring, performance optimization
- [x] **Academic Relations Lead** - Psychology background, research connections, publication experience

**Key Requirements for All Roles:**
- Strong technical skills with demonstrated open-source contributions
- Experience with high-performance web applications
- Understanding of scientific software requirements
- Commitment to precision engineering and quality standards
- Ability to work within mandatory audit gates (Gemini Pro + O3)

**Deliverable**: Complete job descriptions created in `job-descriptions.md`  
**Risk Level**: High (team quality determines project success)  
**Notes**: Job descriptions emphasize precision timing requirements and scientific rigor

### 2. Form Core Development Team  
**Status**: Ready to Start (job descriptions completed)
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 2 weeks  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Hiring Requirements:**
- [ ] Post job descriptions to technical recruitment platforms
- [ ] Conduct initial candidate screening and technical assessments
- [ ] Complete team interviews and cultural fit evaluation
- [ ] Extend offers to selected candidates
- [ ] Plan 30-day onboarding program for new team members

**Dependencies**: Budget approval, job descriptions (✅ completed)
**Risk Level**: High  
**Notes**: Begin hiring process immediately with completed job descriptions

### 3. Create Recruitment Strategy and Platform Postings
**Status**: Not Started  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 1 week  
**Priority**: High  

**Recruitment Strategy Requirements:**
- [ ] Identify target recruitment platforms (AngelList, Stack Overflow, GitHub Jobs, academic networks)
- [ ] Create compelling job postings adapted for each platform
- [ ] Develop technical assessment criteria and coding challenges
- [ ] Plan interview process and evaluation rubrics
- [ ] Establish compensation benchmarks and equity guidelines
- [ ] Create talent sourcing strategy (LinkedIn, GitHub, academic conferences)

**Key Platforms for Posting:**
- **Technical Roles**: Stack Overflow Jobs, GitHub Jobs, AngelList, RemoteOK
- **Academic Role**: Chronicle of Higher Education, Inside Higher Ed, PsyJobSearch
- **General**: LinkedIn, Indeed, company website careers page
- **Specialized**: Y Combinator Work List, RemoteOK, FlexJobs

**Dependencies**: Job descriptions (✅ completed), budget approval
**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Focus on platforms with high-quality technical talent and academic researchers

---

**Status**: Completed

**Completion Date**: July 8, 2025

## Future Sprint Preparations

### 6. Academic Partnership Development
**Status**: Not Started

**Due**: Before Alpha Release

### Currently No Blocked Items
All identified tasks can proceed with appropriate resource allocation.

### Potential Future Blockers

#### Academic Validation Requirements
- **Blocker**: Need IRB approval for validation studies
- **Impact**: Could delay academic publication timeline
- **Mitigation**: Begin IRB process early, have backup validation approaches

#### Hardware Testing Complexity  
- **Blocker**: Specialized timing measurement equipment availability
- **Impact**: Could delay timing precision validation
- **Mitigation**: Partner with existing psychophysics labs for testing

#### Browser API Limitations
- **Blocker**: Web Audio API restrictions or changes
- **Impact**: Could compromise timing precision claims
- **Mitigation**: Maintain fallback timing strategies, desktop app alternative

---

## Resource Requirements

### Development Team (Immediate Need)
- **Senior Frontend Developer** (TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL)
- **Senior Backend Developer** (Node.js, databases, API design)  
- **UI/UX Designer** (accessibility, scientific workflows)
- **DevOps Engineer** (CI/CD, monitoring, deployment)
- **Academic Relations Specialist** (research community connections)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- **Development Hardware**: High-performance workstations for each developer
- **Testing Equipment**: Timing validation hardware suite ($10-15k estimated)
- **Cloud Infrastructure**: CI/CD, monitoring, private npm registry
- **Software Licenses**: Development tools, monitoring services

### Budget Estimates
- **Team (6 months)**: $400-500k (depending on location/seniority)
- **Equipment**: $25-30k (hardware, testing, infrastructure)  
- **Services**: $5-10k (cloud, monitoring, tools)
- **Total Sprint 1-5**: $430-540k estimated

---

## Success Dependencies

### Technical Milestones
1. **Sprint 1**: ≤2ms timing precision demonstrated
2. **Sprint 2**: Complete experiment compiles and executes
3. **Sprint 3**: GUI provides intuitive experiment building
4. **Sprint 5**: Real-time collaboration functional
5. **Sprint 10**: 3 founding labs running production studies

### Strategic Milestones  
1. **Academic Paper**: Timing validation study submitted to peer review
2. **Founding Labs**: 5 research partnerships established
3. **DOI Integration**: Automatic citation system functional
4. **Network Effects**: First viral experiment sharing demonstrated
5. **Market Validation**: Clear demand from research community

---

## Risk Monitoring

### High-Risk Items Requiring Attention
- **Timing Precision Validation**: Single point of failure for entire value proposition
- **Academic Community Adoption**: Without researcher buy-in, technical excellence is meaningless
- **Team Formation**: Need senior developers with specific expertise
- **Browser API Stability**: Dependency on evolving web standards

### Early Warning Indicators
- Timing validation tests failing on any target platform
- Academic advisor feedback indicating skepticism
- Difficulty recruiting qualified development team
- Web API deprecation or restriction announcements

---

*Next tasks updated weekly. Priority items reviewed daily during active development.*

**Current Focus**: Complete sprint designs to enable full project planning and team formation.

# Psycho-Forge Development Team Job Descriptions

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Company:** Psycho-Forge Labs  
**Location:** Remote-first with optional co-working  
**Employment Type:** Full-time, Contract, or Consultant  
**Updated:** July 2025

---

## Mission Statement

We are building the platform that will **end the reproducibility crisis in behavioral research**. Every role demands precision engineering, scientific rigor, and commitment to quality that will define the next era of psychological science.

### Universal Requirements (All Roles)

- **Precision Mindset**: Commitment to ≤2ms timing accuracy and zero-compromise quality
- **Scientific Understanding**: Appreciation for research methodology and scientific integrity
- **Open Source Experience**: Demonstrated contributions to public repositories
- **Quality Gates**: Willingness to work within mandatory AI audit processes (Gemini Pro + O3)
- **Collaborative Spirit**: Experience with remote teams and asynchronous communication
- **Performance Focus**: High-performance web applications and desktop-class experiences

---

## 1. Senior Frontend Developer - Timing Precision Specialist

### Role Overview
Lead the development of the world's most precise web-based experiment platform, achieving sub-2ms timing accuracy that rivals desktop applications.

### Key Responsibilities
- **High-Precision Timing**: Implement Web Audio API clock synchronization and RequestAnimationFrame optimization
- **WebGL/PixiJS Graphics**: Build stimulus rendering engine with frame-perfect accuracy
- **TypeScript Architecture**: Design type-safe, maintainable frontend architecture
- **SvelteKit Development**: Create responsive, accessible user interfaces
- **Performance Optimization**: Achieve 144Hz frame rates with minimal jitter
- **Cross-Browser Testing**: Ensure timing precision across all major browsers
- **Scientific Integration**: Collaborate with researchers to understand timing requirements

### Technical Requirements
- **TypeScript Expert**: 5+ years with complex type systems and generics
- **WebGL/Graphics**: Experience with PixiJS, Three.js, or similar rendering engines
- **Timing Precision**: Knowledge of browser performance APIs and timing optimization
- **SvelteKit Proficiency**: 2+ years with Svelte ecosystem
- **Web Audio API**: Experience with audio timing and synchronization
- **Performance Profiling**: Skills with Chrome DevTools and performance analysis
- **Testing**: Unit testing, integration testing, and timing validation

### Scientific Context
- Understanding of psychophysics and reaction time research
- Experience with scientific software or measurement applications
- Appreciation for reproducibility and precision requirements

### Compensation
- **Salary**: $140,000 - $180,000 (based on experience)
- **Equity**: 0.5% - 1.5% (vesting over 4 years)
- **Benefits**: Health, dental, vision, 401k matching
- **Equipment**: Top-tier development hardware + timing validation equipment

---

## 2. Senior Backend Developer - Scientific Data Architect

### Role Overview
Design and implement the backend infrastructure that handles sensitive research data with enterprise-grade security and performance.

### Key Responsibilities
- **API Design**: Create RESTful APIs for experiment management and data collection
- **Database Architecture**: Design PostgreSQL schemas for scientific data
- **Data Security**: Implement HIPAA-compliant data handling and encryption
- **Performance Optimization**: Handle high-throughput data collection and analysis
- **Integration Services**: Connect with academic systems (LMS, IRB, institutional databases)
- **Export Generation**: Build automated report and data export systems
- **Monitoring**: Implement comprehensive logging and performance monitoring

### Technical Requirements
- **Node.js Expertise**: 5+ years with Express, Fastify, or similar frameworks
- **PostgreSQL Mastery**: Complex queries, performance optimization, and schema design
- **API Design**: RESTful services, OpenAPI documentation, and versioning
- **Security**: OAuth2, JWT, data encryption, and HIPAA compliance
- **Docker/Kubernetes**: Container orchestration and deployment
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions, testing pipelines, and automated deployment
- **Monitoring**: Sentry, New Relic, or similar APM tools

### Scientific Context
- Understanding of research data types and statistical analysis
- Experience with scientific databases or research platforms
- Knowledge of academic institutional requirements

### Compensation
- **Salary**: $130,000 - $170,000 (based on experience)
- **Equity**: 0.5% - 1.5% (vesting over 4 years)
- **Benefits**: Health, dental, vision, 401k matching
- **Equipment**: High-performance development workstation

---

## 3. UI/UX Designer - Accessibility & Scientific Workflows

### Role Overview
Design intuitive interfaces that make complex scientific software accessible to researchers worldwide while maintaining WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.

### Key Responsibilities
- **Accessibility Design**: Ensure 100% WCAG 2.2 AA compliance
- **Scientific Workflows**: Design experiment creation and data analysis interfaces
- **Design Systems**: Create comprehensive component libraries and style guides
- **User Research**: Conduct studies with psychology researchers and students
- **Figma Expertise**: Maintain design systems and collaborate with developers
- **Responsive Design**: Ensure interfaces work across all devices and screen sizes
- **Data Visualization**: Design clear, informative charts and analytics dashboards

### Technical Requirements
- **Figma Mastery**: Advanced prototyping and design system management
- **Accessibility Expert**: WCAG 2.2 AA compliance and screen reader testing
- **HTML/CSS**: Understanding of modern CSS and responsive design
- **Design Systems**: Experience with atomic design and component libraries
- **User Research**: Qualitative and quantitative UX research methods
- **Scientific Software**: Experience with research tools or academic interfaces

### Scientific Context
- Understanding of research methodology and experimental design
- Experience with scientific software (R, Python, MATLAB, etc.)
- Appreciation for precision and detail in research contexts

### Compensation
- **Salary**: $120,000 - $150,000 (based on experience)
- **Equity**: 0.3% - 1.0% (vesting over 4 years)
- **Benefits**: Health, dental, vision, 401k matching
- **Equipment**: Design workstation with professional monitors

---

## 4. DevOps Engineer - Performance & Reliability

### Role Overview
Build and maintain the infrastructure that ensures 99.9% uptime and lightning-fast performance for researchers conducting time-sensitive experiments.

### Key Responsibilities
- **Kubernetes Management**: Deploy and manage containerized applications
- **CI/CD Pipelines**: Automate testing, building, and deployment processes
- **Performance Monitoring**: Implement comprehensive APM and alerting systems
- **Security**: Maintain security scanning, vulnerability management, and compliance
- **Scaling**: Design auto-scaling infrastructure for variable research loads
- **Backup & Recovery**: Implement bulletproof data backup and disaster recovery
- **Cost Optimization**: Monitor and optimize cloud infrastructure costs

### Technical Requirements
- **Kubernetes Expert**: 3+ years with container orchestration
- **CI/CD Mastery**: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar automation platforms
- **Cloud Platforms**: AWS, GCP, or Azure with infrastructure as code
- **Monitoring**: Prometheus, Grafana, DataDog, or similar APM tools
- **Security**: Container security, vulnerability scanning, and compliance
- **Scripting**: Bash, Python, or Go for automation and tooling
- **Database Operations**: PostgreSQL administration and optimization

### Scientific Context
- Understanding of research data sensitivity and compliance requirements
- Experience with high-availability systems for critical applications
- Appreciation for the importance of uptime in research contexts

### Compensation
- **Salary**: $125,000 - $165,000 (based on experience)
- **Equity**: 0.3% - 1.0% (vesting over 4 years)
- **Benefits**: Health, dental, vision, 401k matching
- **Equipment**: DevOps workstation with multiple monitors

---

## 5. Academic Relations Lead - Research Community Connector

### Role Overview
Bridge the gap between cutting-edge technology and academic research, building relationships that drive adoption and validate our scientific claims.

### Key Responsibilities
- **Academic Partnerships**: Identify and establish relationships with 5 founding research labs
- **Publication Strategy**: Plan and execute peer-reviewed publication of platform validation
- **Conference Presence**: Represent Psycho-Forge at psychology and tech conferences
- **User Research**: Conduct interviews and studies with researchers to guide development
- **Documentation**: Create academic-focused documentation and tutorials
- **Community Building**: Foster a community of researchers using the platform
- **Grant Writing**: Assist with NSF, NIH, and other research funding applications

### Academic Requirements
- **Psychology PhD** or equivalent research experience
- **Publication Record**: 5+ peer-reviewed publications in psychology or related fields
- **Conference Experience**: Regular presenter at academic conferences
- **Research Networks**: Existing connections in psychology research community
- **Grant Experience**: Experience with federal research funding applications
- **Communication**: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills

### Technical Understanding
- **Research Software**: Experience with R, Python, MATLAB, or similar tools
- **Experimental Design**: Deep understanding of research methodology
- **Data Analysis**: Statistical analysis and interpretation skills
- **Technology Adoption**: Experience introducing new tools to academic settings

### Compensation
- **Salary**: $110,000 - $140,000 (based on experience)
- **Equity**: 0.5% - 1.5% (vesting over 4 years)
- **Benefits**: Health, dental, vision, 401k matching
- **Travel**: Conference and research visit travel budget

---

## Team Culture & Values

### Work Environment
- **Remote-First**: Flexible work arrangements with optional co-working spaces
- **Asynchronous**: Deep work time with planned collaboration windows
- **Documentation**: Everything important is written down and searchable
- **Continuous Learning**: Budget for courses, conferences, and skill development
- **Open Source**: Contributions to open source projects encouraged and supported

### Quality Standards
- **Audit Gates**: All code must pass Gemini Pro and O3 AI audits before sprint completion
- **Testing**: >90% code coverage required for all production code
- **Performance**: Sub-2ms timing precision is non-negotiable
- **Accessibility**: 100% WCAG 2.2 AA compliance required
- **Security**: Enterprise-grade security practices and regular audits

### Growth Opportunities
- **Technical Leadership**: Opportunity to shape the technical direction of the platform
- **Academic Impact**: Contribute to research that could transform behavioral science
- **Conference Speaking**: Support for conference presentations and thought leadership
- **Open Source**: Create tools and libraries that benefit the broader community
- **Equity Growth**: Significant equity upside as the platform grows

---

## Application Process

### Step 1: Initial Application
- **Resume/CV**: Technical background and relevant experience
- **Cover Letter**: Why you're excited about ending the reproducibility crisis
- **Portfolio**: Links to relevant work (GitHub, publications, designs, etc.)
- **Code Sample**: For technical roles, provide a code sample with explanation

### Step 2: Technical Assessment
- **Frontend**: Build a simple timing-sensitive web application
- **Backend**: Design an API for scientific data management
- **Design**: Create mockups for a scientific workflow interface
- **DevOps**: Design infrastructure for a high-availability research platform
- **Academic**: Develop a plan for validating timing precision claims

### Step 3: Team Interviews
- **Technical Interview**: Deep dive into your technical expertise
- **Culture Interview**: Alignment with mission and team values
- **Scenario Interview**: How you'd handle specific project challenges
- **Final Interview**: Meet with founders and discuss long-term vision

### Step 4: References & Offer
- **References**: Professional references from previous roles
- **Offer**: Competitive package with equity and benefits
- **Onboarding**: Comprehensive 30-day onboarding program

---

## How to Apply

**Email**: careers@psycho-forge.com  
**Subject**: [Role Name] - [Your Name]  
**Application Deadline**: Rolling basis, positions filled when right candidate found

### Questions?
- **Technical Questions**: dev@psycho-forge.com
- **Academic Questions**: research@psycho-forge.com
- **General Questions**: info@psycho-forge.com

---

## Equal Opportunity

Psycho-Forge is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of race, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic.

---

**Ready to end the reproducibility crisis? Join us.**

*"We are building the platform that will define the next era of behavioral science. Every line of code, every design decision, and every academic partnership brings us closer to a world where every research finding is perfectly reproducible."*

- The Psycho-Forge Team

**Status:** Ready for Implementation

---

## Executive Summary

### Target Timeline
- **Week 1**: Platform postings go live
- **Week 2-3**: Active candidate sourcing and screening
- **Week 4-5**: Technical assessments and interviews

- Performance optimization techniques

## Risk Mitigation Strategies

### High-Risk Scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Low Application Volume
**Risk**: Insufficient qualified candidates apply  
**Probability**: Medium  
**Impact**: High - delays team formation

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Increase active sourcing efforts (double LinkedIn outreach)
- Expand platform mix to include RemoteOK, HackerNews
- Leverage academic networks for cross-promotion
- Increase referral bonuses to $7,500
- Consider remote-first international candidates

#### Scenario 2: High Candidate Rejection Rate
**Risk**: Candidates decline offers due to compensation/equity  
**Probability**: Medium  
**Impact**: High - need to restart recruitment

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Benchmark compensation weekly against market rates
- Emphasize equity upside and mission impact
- Offer flexible compensation (more equity vs. salary)
- Provide signing bonuses for exceptional candidates
- Accelerate timeline to reduce competitor influence

#### Scenario 3: Technical Assessment Failure
**Risk**: Candidates can't meet timing precision requirements  
**Probability**: High  
**Impact**: Medium - need broader candidate pool

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Create assessment training materials and resources
- Offer feedback and retry opportunities for close candidates
- Adjust requirements to focus on learning ability vs. current skills
- Provide take-home timeline flexibility
- Consider junior-to-senior progression path

#### Scenario 4: Cultural Misfit
**Risk**: Hired candidates don't align with quality standards  
**Probability**: Low  
**Impact**: Very High - compromises entire project

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Extend probationary period to 90 days
- Implement weekly 1:1s during first month
- Require AI audit gate passage within first sprint
- Create clear performance metrics and feedback loops
- Maintain bench of backup candidates

### Contingency Plans

#### Plan A: Extended Timeline
If recruitment takes longer than 8 weeks:
- Continue with partial team (3/5 roles filled)
- Use contract developers for specific components
- Delay Sprint 1 start by 2-4 weeks maximum
- Maintain quality standards over speed

#### Plan B: Remote International
If US/Canada talent pool insufficient:
- Expand to European timezone candidates
- Adjust compensation for location-based cost differences
- Ensure English proficiency and communication skills
- Consider visa sponsorship for exceptional candidates

#### Plan C: Junior + Senior Pairing
If senior talent unavailable:
- Hire senior lead + junior developer pairs
- Implement mentorship and training programs
- Adjust timeline expectations for ramp-up
- Maintain quality through code review and AI audits

## Risk Mitigation Strategies

### High-Risk Scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Low Application Volume
**Risk**: Insufficient qualified candidates apply  
**Probability**: Medium  
**Impact**: High - delays team formation

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Increase active sourcing efforts (double LinkedIn outreach)
- Expand platform mix to include RemoteOK, HackerNews
- Leverage academic networks for cross-promotion
- Increase referral bonuses to $7,500
- Consider remote-first international candidates

#### Scenario 2: High Candidate Rejection Rate
**Risk**: Candidates decline offers due to compensation/equity  
**Probability**: Medium  
**Impact**: High - need to restart recruitment

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Benchmark compensation weekly against market rates
- Emphasize equity upside and mission impact

- Accelerate timeline to reduce competitor influence

#### Scenario 3: Technical Assessment Failure
**Risk**: Candidates can't meet timing precision requirements

**Impact**: Medium - need broader candidate pool

- Provide take-home timeline flexibility
- Consider junior-to-senior progression path

#### Scenario 4: Cultural Misfit
**Risk**: Hired candidates don't align with quality standards  
**Probability**: Low  
**Impact**: Very High - compromises entire project

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Extend probationary period to 90 days
- Implement weekly 1:1s during first month
- Require AI audit gate passage within first sprint
- Create clear performance metrics and feedback loops
- Maintain bench of backup candidates

- Continue with partial team (3/5 roles filled)
- Use contract developers for specific components
- Delay Sprint 1 start by 2-4 weeks maximum
- Maintain quality standards over speed

#### Plan B: Remote International
If US/Canada talent pool insufficient:
- Expand to European timezone candidates
- Adjust compensation for location-based cost differences
- Ensure English proficiency and communication skills
- Consider visa sponsorship for exceptional candidates

#### Plan C: Junior + Senior Pairing
If senior talent unavailable:
- Hire senior lead + junior developer pairs
- Implement mentorship and training programs
- Adjust timeline expectations for ramp-up
- Maintain quality through code review and AI audits

### 🏗️ 1. High-Precision Clock Implementation (KER-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 1 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- [ ] Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- [ ] Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- [ ] Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/timing/
├── src/
│   ├── PrecisionClock.ts
│   ├── TimingDriftDetector.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
└── package.json
```

**Dependencies**: Nx monorepo setup, testing infrastructure  
**Risk Level**: High (foundational for entire platform)  
**Notes**: This is the critical first implementation for ≤2ms timing precision

**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- [ ] Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- [ ] Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- [ ] Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/timing/
├── src/
│   ├── PrecisionClock.ts
│   ├── TimingDriftDetector.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
└── package.json
```
